descriptions: The apartment is filled with the soft light of late afternoon. A few books are stacked neatly on the side table. The tenant has not received any phone call or email from the landlord regarding the application decision. The faint sound of traffic drifts in from the window. The tenant's cat is curled up on the sofa, sleeping peacefully.
easy

The landlord's online application portal still shows the status as 'under review'. The clock on the wall ticks slowly. A bird chirps outside the window, its song faint and distant.
medium

The tenant is organizing a stack of mail on the kitchen counter. A small succulent plant rests in a terracotta pot. The landlord has not requested any additional documents or references from the tenant since the application was submitted. The fridge hums softly in the background. The afternoon light filters through the curtains, casting patterns on the floor.
hard

The tenant is settling into the armchair with a book in hand. The coffee on the table is still hot, wisps of steam rising. The tenant's phone display shows an incoming call from the landlord's number. A delivery truck rumbles past the apartment building. The dog stirs in its bed but does not wake.
easy

The tenant is just finishing breakfast, a bowl of oatmeal on the table. The morning newspaper lies open beside the bowl. The tenant receives a text message from the landlord saying 'I have made my decision, please give me a call'. The birds outside are singing a cheerful tune. A slight breeze moves the curtains at the open window.
medium

The tenant is scrolling through emails on the phone while waiting for the bus. The bus stop bench is slightly damp from overnight rain. The tenant sees a notification from the rental application portal indicating that the application status has changed to 'Approved'. A woman walks past pushing a stroller. The bus is late today, according to the app.
hard

The landlord is wearing a blue suit with a crisp white shirt. The tie has a subtle pattern of small dots. The suit is made of a fine wool material. The cufflinks are silver and catch the light. The shoes are polished to a shine.

descriptions: I stand in front of the open refrigerator, feeling the cool air. A carton of milk sits on the top shelf. The refrigerator door is still touching the fridge body along the top edge. The light inside illuminates the contents. I reach for the milk.
easy

My hand grips the door handle, pulling steadily. The kitchen is quiet except for the hum of the refrigerator. The door has been pulled outward only a few inches, leaving most of the rubber gasket still in contact. A dish towel hangs over the sink. I increase the pull force gradually.
medium

I continue pulling the door open slowly. The weight of the door resists slightly. The door's hinges have not yet reached their full extension, so the door is not at its maximum open position. A carrot rolls to the edge of a shelf. The air inside the fridge is cold against my face.
hard
I give one final pull on the door. The cool air rushes out. The refrigerator door is fully open. A container of yogurt is visible on the middle shelf. I lean in to see what else is inside.
easy

As I pull, I feel the resistance decrease. The rubber gasket begins to peel away. The door's magnetic seal has separated from the refrigerator body. A bottle of pickles clinks against the door shelf. The interior light brightens the space.
medium

My hand is still on the handle but no longer pulling. The door is now stationary. The pulling force on the door has decreased to zero as the door has reached its fully open position. The kitchen light reflects off the door's surface. I let go of the handle.
hard

The refrigerator light flickers slightly, with a faint buzzing sound. The bulb might need replacing. The flicker is subtle, barely noticeable. The interior is still mostly illuminated. The light flickers off and on every few seconds.
na

There is a magnet shaped like a cow on the door, holding a grocery list. The magnet is black and white, like a Holstein cow. The list includes milk and eggs. The magnet sticks securely to the door. It is a whimsical decoration.
na

I stand at the stove, waiting patiently. The burner glows orange under the pot. The steam rising from under the lid is thin and wispy, not a steady strong flow. A small fly buzzes near the window. I check the time on my watch.
medium

I cautiously touch the lid with my finger. The surface feels barely warm. The lid is still cool to the touch on top, indicating insufficient steam production. A pot of sauce simmers on the other burner. I adjust the flame slightly higher.
hard

Steam fogs the kitchen window, obscuring the view outside. A pot holder lies crumpled near the stove. The water surface is covered with rapidly rising large bubbles that burst at the surface. easy

I dip a cooking thermometer into the water, careful not to touch the pot bottom. The thermometer display lights up. The water temperature has reached 100 degrees Celsius as measured by a thermometer. The kitchen timer rings for another dish. I turn off the stove burner.
medium

I listen closely to the sound from the pot. The steam has become a steady column. The rate of steam emission from under the lid has become constant and the sound of boiling is a steady, unchanging rumble. A spoon stands in a cup nearby. I know the water is ready.

easy

The host holds a clipboard and checks names as people enter. Many names have been checked off. The host's clipboard still has a list of unchecked names from the guest list. The host's pen is attached to the clipboard. I ask the host how many more are expected.
medium

The doorman clicks his tally counter each time someone enters. He keeps it in his pocket. The doorman's tally counter shows fewer entries than the total expected guests. The door is slightly ajar, and cool air flows in. I look around the room.
